
### exercise  {.smaller}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column}

vulnerable code:
```cpp
std::istream *in =
    new std::ifstream("in.txt");
...
delete in;
...
char *other_buffer =
    new char[strlen(INPUT) + 1];
strcpy(other_buffer, INPUT);
...
char c = in->get();
```

:::

::: {.column}

ifstream internals:
```cpp
class istream {
    ...
    int get() { ... buf->uflow(); ... }
    streambuf *buf;
    ~istream() { delete buf; }
};
class streambuf {
    ...
protected:
    virtual type_for_char uflow() = 0;
        /* called to get next char*/
};
class _File_streambuf : public streambuf { ... }
```

:::

::::
 

* attacker goal: change what uflow() call does
* Q1: [ assuming same size $\rightarrow$ likely to get same address]{.my-small}, what size for attacker to choose for <code>INPUT</code>?
* Q2: where in INPUT to place pointer to code to run?
